codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers feature description: BM25 keyword search over structured conversation logs with temporal query support. All 8 tests pass. Code quality is good.

issues.description: Recall module is not re-exported via pub use. Consumers must use fully qualified path swell_memory::recall::RecallService rather than swell_memory::RecallService. Consider adding 'pub use recall::{ConversationLog, ConversationAction, RecallQuery, RecallResult, Bm25Params, RecallService};' to lib.rs if public API is desired.

Duplicate schema initialization: init_conversation_logs_schema() is called from both SqliteMemoryStore::init_schema() and recall.rs:SqliteMemoryStore::init_conversation_logs_schema(). The recall.rs static method exists but is redundant since init_schema() handles it. Could be removed to reduce duplication, though it doesn't cause functional issues since CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker attempted to read .factory/services.yaml but the file doesn't exist. Transcript shows: 'Error: Error reading file: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access '/Users/ludwigjonsson/.factory/services.yaml''. This is consistent across all memory feature workers. The services.yaml file should either be created with documented services or AGENTS.md should clarify that it doesn't exist yet.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows File read for '.factory/services.yaml' failed with ENOENT. Worker proceeded without this file.

summary: Feature memory-recall passes scrutiny. Implementation correctly provides: (1) ConversationLog struct for agent interactions with all expected fields (id, session_id, task_id, agent_id, agent_role, action, content, timestamp, metadata); (2) BM25 keyword search via tokenize/extract_terms/calculate_bm25_score with configurable k1/b parameters; (3) Temporal queries via start_time/end_time filters; (4) RecallService high-level API. All 8 tests pass. Build and clippy clean. No blocking issues found.
